title: Bhature

overview.description: Soft, fluffy North Indian deep-fried bread traditionally served with Punjabi Chole. Crisp outside with a light, airy interior.

method: Mix together the plain flour, baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, sugar and salt.
Add the yoghurt and vegetable oil.
Mix into a soft dough, adding warm water a little at a time if needed.
Knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Roll each into an oval or circle about 3–4mm thick.
Heat the frying oil to approximately 180°C.
Carefully lower one bhatura into the oil.
Gently press it beneath the surface with a slotted spoon until it puffs.
Turn and fry for about 30 seconds on the second side.
Remove and drain on kitchen paper.

learning.did-you-know: Bhature and Chole are one of North India's most iconic food pairings, especially around Delhi and Punjab.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a soft enriched dough and deep fry bread until it puffs into a light, airy bhatura.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: The traditional accompaniment to Chole and a great recipe for occasional weekend meals.
chapman-tips: Keep the oil hot enough for the bhature to puff quickly without absorbing excess oil.
Don't stack them immediately after frying or they'll soften.
A soft dough produces the lightest texture.
Serve as soon as possible after frying.
notes.batch-cooking: Not recommended. Bhature are at their best freshly fried.
notes.leftovers: Can be reheated in a hot oven, although they lose some of their characteristic puffiness.
